Name: Space Engineering Center (SEC)
Relevance: Primary hub for satellite design, hosting the TAU-SAT nanosatellite series and ground station operations.

Name: Raymond & Beverly Sackler School of Physics & Astronomy
Relevance: Conducts fundamental research in astrophysics, cosmology, and observational astronomy.

Name: The Florence and George Wise Observatory
Relevance: Israel's professional astronomical observatory; used for exoplanet, supernova, and asteroid research.

Name: Remote Sensing Laboratory (RSL)
Relevance: Specializes in hyperspectral imaging and Earth observation from satellite and airborne platforms.

Name: Small Satellite Laboratory
Relevance: Focuses on the engineering and development of CubeSats and nanosatellite components (TAUSAT-1/2/3).

Name: Plasma and Pulsed Power Research (Prof. Isak Beilis)
Relevance: Research on vacuum arcs and plasma phenomena directly applicable to plasma thrusters and electric propulsion.

Name: Department of Geophysics and Planetary Sciences
Relevance: Researches planetary atmospheres, solar system evolution, and planetary geology.

Name: Elrom Center for Air and Space Power
Relevance: Joint initiative with the IAF focusing on strategy, policy, and multidisciplinary research in air and space power.

Name: Space Materials and Durability Lab (Prof. Noam Eliaz)
Relevance: Develops materials resistant to the harsh space environment, including radiation-grade coatings and corrosion resistance.

Name: TAU-SAT Student Design Team
Relevance: The primary student organization responsible for designing, building, and operating the university's CubeSats.

Name: National Committee for Space Research (NCSR)
Relevance: The 1960s precursor to the Israel Space Agency; originated at TAU and launched the Shavit-2 rocket in 1961.

Name: TEVEL Program
Relevance: Educational satellite program hosted by TAU in collaboration with the Israel Space Agency.

Name: Prof. Pavel Ginzburg Lab (UAV Physics)
Relevance: Research into radar signature reduction and electromagnetic scattering for UAV and aerospace applications.

Name: Curiosity Robotics Lab
Relevance: Develops autonomous systems and AI algorithms meaningfully adjacent to space-borne robotic exploration.

Name: Space Communications Lab (Prof. Itzhak Tamo)
Relevance: Research into coding and information theory specifically for satellite data storage and communication.

Name: Ilan Ramon Youth Physics Center
Relevance: Space and astronomy outreach center named after Israel's first astronaut, affiliated with TAU School of Physics.
